architecture_summary: BiFormer is a hierarchical vision transformer built around Bi-level Routing Attention (BRA), which first prunes attention at a coarse region level with top-k routing and then applies fine-grained token-to-token attention over the union of routed regions. It uses overlapped patch embedding, patch merging, depthwise convolution, and an MLP in a four-stage pyramid backbone.

novel_contributions: We propose a novel dynamic sparse attention via bi-level routing to enable a more flexible allocation of computations with content awareness.

We provide a simple yet effective implementation of the proposed bi-level routing attention, which utilizes the sparsity to save both computation and memory while involving only GPU-friendly dense matrix multiplications.
Built with the proposed bi-level routing attention, a new general vision transformer, named BiFormer, is then presented.
We show that BiFormer achieves a better computation-performance trade-off and strong results on image classification, object detection, instance segmentation, and semantic segmentation.

key_claims.claim: BiFormer uses a query-adaptive bi-level routing attention mechanism that first filters irrelevant key-value pairs at a coarse region level and then applies fine-grained token-to-token attention in routed regions.
BiFormer-T achieves 83.8% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet-1K with 4.6G FLOPs, which the paper says is the best under similar computation budgets without external data or distillation.
BiFormer-S achieves 83.8% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet-1K and BiFormer-B achieves 84.3% top-1 accuracy, outperforming other methods in their FLOPs groups.
On COCO 2017, BiFormer-S and BiFormer-B show strong object detection and instance segmentation results, with BiFormer-B reaching 48.6 mAP and 43.7 mAP^m.
On ADE20K, BiFormer-S and BiFormer-B achieve 49.8 and 51.0 Upernet mIoU(%), respectively, with similar gains under Semantic FPN.
BRA has significantly better performance than existing sparse attention mechanisms in the ablation study, for both image classification and semantic segmentation.
The paper analyzes BRA and states that it achieves O((HW)^{3/2}) complexity with a proper region partition size.
